description: One hash-chained move in a Legba span log. Chain-bearing (prev_hash/record_hash intra-span chain), CAS-referencing (input/output hashes), canonical-size-capped at 4 KB. Not crypto-bearing: moves are not individually signed — the gate token signs the span head (GateToken).

move_kind.description: Discriminator literal pinning the SpanMove shape.

contract_version.description: Hounfour contract version. Pinned for the Legba cycle-1 ship line; the merge train assigns the final literal.

run_id.description: Composition run identifier (consumer-shaped). Binds the move to one run; tokens and the receipt carry the same id.

span_index.description: Zero-based segment index within the run.

seq.description: Strictly monotonic move sequence within the span (LM-1). Gaps and repeats are chain violations.

prev_hash.description: sha256:<64-hex> of the prior move's canonical form; the first move of a span carries the genesis anchor (LEGBA_GENESIS_ANCHOR). LM-2 verifies chain continuity.

kind.description: Event class: a tool invocation or a model emission (narration, judgment, verdict).

determinism.description: Verification regime (LG-5). `re_executable` REQUIRES the tool to be content-recording allowlisted (replay needs retention); unlisted and nondeterministic tools are `attestable` by construction. Misclassification is the substrate's main integrity lie — the determinism manifest lint owns it.

tool.description: Tool name for kind=tool (per the determinism manifest).

input_hash.description: Bare 64-hex sha256 over the canonical input bytes (composition rule in the schema header). CAS reference (`x-cas-reference`).

output_hash.description: Bare 64-hex sha256 over the canonical output bytes. CAS reference; re-execution divergence on an allowlisted re_executable move is a machine-detectable fraud proof (LG-4).

env_fingerprint.description: Bare 64-hex sha256 of the tool version + environment manifest (LG-4b). REQUIRED when determinism=re_executable (LM-4); replay under a divergent environment downgrades to attestable for that challenge — never false fraud.

ts.description: ISO 8601 UTC, microsecond precision, Z suffix (no offset form) — format pinned because ts participates in the canonical bytes.

record_hash.description: Bare 64-hex sha256 of the RFC 8785 canonical JSON of this record with the record_hash key ABSENT (LM-3). The span head is the final move's record_hash.
